name: Spaghetti Squash (Vegan)
recipeIngredient: 4 to 8 ounces sun-dried tomatoes - soften in a bowl of boiling water for no more than two (2) minutes.
2 to 4 shallots (more or less to taste), chopped
2 to 4 cloves fresh garlic (more or less to taste), chopped
1/2 or more red peppers, coarsely chopped
Lemon pepper and oregano to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Split the squash lengthwise with a heavy knife (careful). Scrape out and discard seeds.
- Easiest cooking method is in the microwave, but baking is also acceptable.
2. For microwave: Place inside up in a rectangular glass dish. Add 1/4 cup of water to the dish.
3. Cover with plastic wrap and cook on high for about 10 minutes, or until just "al dente."
- While cooking, prepare the sauce:
a. Sauté garlic and shallots in olive oil with chopped green bell pepper until just tender—do not brown.
b. If using fresh red peppers, add them now.
c. Coarsely chop tomatoes (canned if used) and add to the pan with cumin, lemon pepper, and oregano.
4. Add basil leaves and balsamic vinegar at the last minute, cooking only until the basil just wilts.
5. To assemble: Scrape the squash halves horizontally to free the "spaghetti." Place in a serving dish or large shallow pasta bowl. Pour the sauce over the squash and fold it in. Serve immediately. The dish keeps well and can be reheated in the microwave.
Note: Using too much bell pepper may overpower the dish.
